steps.title: Spot the contrasting approaches
steps.content: The passage sets up two different ways of understanding space. The first part describes the quantitative analytical approach using GIS spatial technologies. The second part introduces Doreen Massey, who defines space through socio-political forces. The word “instead” at the end of the sentence is the key signal — it shows Massey's approach is fundamentally different from, not similar to, the quantitative method.

steps.title: Match the transition to the contrast
steps.content: ”by contrast,” directly signals the opposition between these two approaches — one technical and quantitative, the other qualitative and socio-political. The other three choices suggest either similarity (likewise), exemplification (for example), or logical consequence (as such), none of which fit the opposing relationship. That matches choice B — B is correct.
